content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6-max

## Summary

Both amendments identify real, verifiable issues. Amendment fr-2-ed6d00c2 correctly identifies that bead ralph-burning-lmm references three source paths that do not exist (src/contexts/engine/, src/contexts/cli/, src/contexts/conformance/), while the actual code lives at src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs, src/cli/, and src/contexts/conformance_spec/. Amendment fr-2-a1b1458b correctly identifies that the tracked result symlink points to a machine-local /nix/store/ path, which is meaningless churn that will break on other machines.

## Votes

- `fr-2-ed6d00c2`: Accept
  Verified: all three paths listed in the bead's Files section are wrong. src/contexts/engine/ does not exist (actual: src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs), src/contexts/cli/ does not exist (actual: src/cli/), and src/contexts/conformance/ does not exist (actual: src/contexts/conformance_spec/). Since this bead is meant to guide profiling work, incorrect file pointers in the authoritative backlog will waste the implementor's time. The fix is straightforward — update the paths to match reality.
- `fr-2-a1b1458b`: Accept
  Verified: result is a symlink to /nix/store/wag10sk2src41jc50maj02q7svn03m0z-..., a machine-local Nix build artifact. The file is listed in .gitignore at line 5, confirming the intent to not track it, yet it remains tracked. Committing store-path updates is pure churn — the path is valid only on the build machine and will be a dangling symlink on every other checkout. The symlink change should be dropped from this branch, and ideally the file should be untracked entirely.
